# clo_dbaas_cluster (Resource)

Manage a managed-database (dbaas) cluster in the project. `enabled` toggles the cluster's power state (start/stop). Databases are managed with the separate `clo_dbaas_database` resource.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up an available datastore (database engine + version) for the project.
data "clo_dbaas_datastores" "all" {
  project_id = "1e8ff0f7-0b8c-4ec5-a0a4-e30cea0db287"
}

# A minimal managed-database cluster. An address is allocated automatically.
resource "clo_dbaas_cluster" "cluster_1" {
  project_id   = "1e8ff0f7-0b8c-4ec5-a0a4-e30cea0db287"
  name         = "app-db"
  datastore_id = data.clo_dbaas_datastores.all.result[0].id
  storage_size = 20

  flavor {
    vcpus = 2
    ram   = 4
  }
}

# A cluster bound to an existing address, created stopped.
resource "clo_dbaas_cluster" "cluster_2" {
  project_id   = "1e8ff0f7-0b8c-4ec5-a0a4-e30cea0db287"
  name         = "reporting-db"
  datastore_id = data.clo_dbaas_datastores.all.result[0].id
  storage_size = 50
  enabled      = false

  flavor {
    vcpus = 4
    ram   = 8
  }

  # Bind an existing address; omit the block to allocate one automatically.
  address {
    id = "b0298e0c-057c-4298-8505-5e57f5a60f8c"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `flavor` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Compute flavor for the cluster nodes (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--flavor))
- `name` (String) Human-readable name of the cluster
- `project_id` (String) ID of the project where the cluster should be created
- `storage_size` (Number) Size of the data storage in GiB. Can only be grown, never shrunk.

### Optional

- `address` (Block List, Max: 1) Address to attach to the cluster. If omitted, one is allocated automatically (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--address))
- `backup_enabled` (Boolean) Whether scheduled backups are enabled for the cluster. Defaults to true.
- `datastore_id` (String) ID of the datastore (database engine + version) for the cluster. Resolve it with the `clo_dbaas_datastores` data source.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the cluster is powered on. Defaults to true.
- `restore_from_backup_id` (String) ID of a backup to restore into the new cluster. Mutually exclusive with an initial database.
- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `backup_hour` (Number) UTC hour at which scheduled backups run
- `created_in` (String) Timestamp the cluster was created
- `databases_count` (Number) Number of databases in the cluster
- `datastore_name` (String) Database engine of the selected datastore
- `datastore_version` (String) Database engine version of the selected datastore
- `external_address` (String) External address of the cluster
- `id` (String) ID of the cluster
- `internal_address` (String) Internal address of the cluster
- `nodes_count` (Number) Number of nodes in the cluster
- `status` (String) Lifecycle status of the cluster
- `storage_used_kb` (Number) Storage currently used, in KiB
- `switch_status` (String) Power switch position reported by the API (`ON`/`OFF`)
- `system_disk_size` (Number) System disk size in GiB

<a id="nestedblock--flavor"></a>
### Nested Schema for `flavor`

Required:

- `ram` (Number) Amount of RAM in GiB
- `vcpus` (Number) Number of virtual CPUs

Read-Only:

- `disk` (Number) System disk size in GiB reported by the API


<a id="nestedblock--address"></a>
### Nested Schema for `address`

Optional:

- `ddos_protection` (Boolean) Whether the allocated address should be DDoS-protected
- `id` (String) Use an existing address with this ID


<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String)
- `delete` (String)
- `read` (String)
- `update` (String)
